THOUGHT:
The search results successfully returned the foundational paper by Recht et al. (2019) that directly investigates the generalization gap of ImageNet classifiers on newly collected test data. This paper is highly relevant to the workshop theme as it empirically demonstrates how academic benchmark success often fails to translate to real-world robustness due to subtle distributional shifts and benchmark overfitting. It perfectly fulfills the remaining citation needs: crediting benchmark datasets (#3), comparing controlled vs. real-world findings (#4), highlighting evaluation methodology gaps (#5), and supporting arguments for continuous validation (#7). I will select index 0 and update the description accordingly.
